_meta.description: Complete defect definitions for all RefrigiWear product categories. Each defect contains checks mapped to all 10 manufacturing process stages.

defects.name: Shell Fabric Delamination
defects.description: Outer nylon shell separating from inner lining or insulation layer due to adhesive or bonding failure.
defects.root_cause: Incorrect bonding adhesive type; excess heat during pressing; substandard shell fabric denier; contaminated substrate before bonding.

development_samples.check: Perform adhesion peel test on proto shell panels. Validate bonding adhesive type and temperature spec. Define shell-to-lining bond strength minimum in tech pack (min 3.0 N/cm).
development_samples.pass_criteria: Bond strength ≥ 3.0 N/cm across all panel locations

size_set.check: Confirm production fabric lot matches approved proto sample shell. Re-run adhesion test on size set bulk fabric. Flag any lot variation.

pp_samples.check: Full construction audit of PP sample. Pull test all shell panels. Confirm bonding process parameters (temperature, pressure, dwell time) are locked in production SOP.
pp_samples.pass_criteria: No delamination under 5 N/cm pull. SOP signed off.

material.check: Incoming roll inspection for pre-laminated shell: check for bubbling, edge delamination, pinholes. Random peel test 1 sample per roll per lot.
material.pass_criteria: Zero delamination on incoming roll inspection

cutting.check: After cutting, check cut panel edges for any delamination triggered by blade heat or tension. Inspect perimeter of cut panels.
cutting.pass_criteria: No edge peeling on any cut panel

assembly.check: After pressing/bonding operations, tactile and visual check of assembled panels for bubbling or edge lift. Every 25 pieces.
assembly.pass_criteria: Zero bubble or edge lift on all inspected panels

inline_inspection.check: Every 50 pcs: pull test on shell seam area, visual check for panel delamination at stress points (underarm, pocket corners).

final_inspection.check: Per AQL 1.0 sampling plan: pull test on finished garment shell panels. Bubble check under light table. Record defect location on defect map.
final_inspection.pass_criteria: Zero critical (delamination) findings within AQL 1.0 sample

packing.check: Visual check of each garment before folding for any obvious shell bubbling or peeling that may have developed during handling.

defects.name: Insulation Clumping / Cold Spots
defects.description: RefrigiFill™ polyester insulation bunching unevenly inside baffles, creating thin areas with reduced thermal performance.
defects.root_cause: Insufficient quilting tack points; poor insulation cutting uniformity; insulation weight below spec; wash-cycle migration.

development_samples.check: Validate quilting pattern and tack point density on proto. Weigh insulation per panel against spec (must be 11.25 oz/yd² ±5%). Light-table inspect for even distribution.
development_samples.pass_criteria: Insulation weight within ±5% of spec. Zero visible thin spots on light table.

size_set.check: Check insulation distribution across all sizes in size set. Larger sizes require more insulation — verify cut templates scaled correctly.
size_set.pass_criteria: Each size panel meets weight spec. Even distribution on light table.

pp_samples.check: Shake test 10 PP sample garments vigorously. Light-table inspect all panels. Wash test 3 pcs (5 cycles) and re-inspect for insulation migration.
pp_samples.pass_criteria: No clumping after shake or wash test. Weight variation <10%.

material.check: Weigh insulation rolls per delivery. Check roll consistency (weight per linear meter). Reject rolls deviating >±5% from spec 11.25 oz/yd².
material.pass_criteria: All incoming insulation rolls within ±5% weight spec

cutting.check: Measure cut insulation panels against master pattern for size accuracy. Check insulation not compressing under cutter weight causing under-cut panels.
cutting.pass_criteria: All cut insulation panels within ±3mm of pattern template

sewing_stitching.check: Inspect quilting stitch pattern for correct spacing (per approved sample). Ensure all quilt channels are complete — no skipped tack points. Check SPI on quilt rows.
sewing_stitching.pass_criteria: Quilt spacing matches approved sample ±5mm. SPI 8-10 per spec.

assembly.check: During panel joining, light-table inspect each assembled panel section before closing seam. Flag any insulation-thin areas to rework station.
assembly.pass_criteria: Zero visible cold spots on light table inspection

inline_inspection.check: Every hour / every 50 pcs: light-table inspect finished garment for cold spots. Shake test 5 pcs per hour. Tactile press all panels.
inline_inspection.pass_criteria: Zero cold spots on inline sample

final_inspection.check: Per AQL 2.5: light table + shake test all sampled garments. Tactile check entire back panel, chest panels, sleeves. Defect map any findings.
final_inspection.pass_criteria: Zero Major (cold spot) findings within AQL 2.5 sample

packing.check: Avoid compressing insulated garments under heavy stacking in cartons. Use box inserts or hang storage where possible. Note on packing instruction.
packing.pass_criteria: Garments packed per approved packing spec (no heavy compression)

defects.name: Color Fading / Shade Variation
defects.description: Shade variation between fabric panels or production lots. Premature fading of shell color or HiVis fluorescent color.
defects.root_cause: Dye lot variation between fabric rolls; incorrect dye concentration; insufficient light fastness; mixed lots in same order cut.

development_samples.check: Create approved lab-dip / color standard. Specify dye lot tolerances. Conduct light fastness test (AATCC 16 min Grade 4). Set acceptable shade range.
development_samples.pass_criteria: Light fastness min Grade 4 (AATCC 16). Shade approved to lab-dip standard.

size_set.check: Verify all size set fabric rolls are from same dye lot. Compare each roll to approved standard under D65 light. Record dye lot numbers.
size_set.pass_criteria: All rolls from same dye lot OR shade difference ≤ Grade 4 vs standard

pp_samples.check: All PP sample fabric must be from approved bulk lot. Submit shade sign-off comparison card. Reject if shade varies across panels in same garment.
pp_samples.pass_criteria: PP sample shade matches approved standard. No panel-to-panel variation visible.

material.check: On receipt: measure each roll end-to-end shade using spectrophotometer. Flag rolls with CIE ΔE > 1.0 vs approved standard. Do NOT mix dye lots in one order.
material.pass_criteria: All rolls ΔE ≤ 1.0. Single dye lot per order.

cutting.check: Sort and mark cut panels by dye lot. Ensure all panels for one garment come from same dye lot. Visual shade check under light box before bundling.
cutting.pass_criteria: 100% single lot per garment bundle. Zero cross-lot mixing.

sewing_stitching.check: Operators to flag any panel shade variation before sewing. Supervisor shade check on first piece of each bundle under D65 lighting.
sewing_stitching.pass_criteria: No shade variation between assembled panels on first piece

assembly.check: After full garment assembly, check all major panels (front, back, sleeves) for shade consistency under D65 light. Flag any mixed-lot garments.
assembly.pass_criteria: No visible shade variation on any assembled garment

inline_inspection.check: Every 2 hours: pull 5 garments and check shade under D65 light. Record dye lot of fabric being run. Compare to approved shade bar.
inline_inspection.pass_criteria: All sampled garments match approved shade bar within Grade 4

final_inspection.check: Per AQL 2.5: shade check all sampled garments under D65 light against approved shade bar. Special attention to side-by-side panel matching.
final_inspection.pass_criteria: All garments within Grade 4 vs standard. No panel variation visible.

packing.check: When packing assorted colors per carton, verify correct colors per packing list. Check no shade-failed garments mixed into approved cartons.
packing.pass_criteria: Correct colors per packing list. No shade-rejected garments in packed cartons.

defects.name: DWR Water-Repellency Failure
defects.description: DWR (Durable Water Repellent) treatment failing to bead water. Garment absorbs moisture instead of repelling it.
defects.root_cause: Low DWR concentration in finishing bath; improper curing temperature or time; fabric pre-treatment contamination; worn-off DWR from factory handling.

development_samples.check: Test DWR on proto fabric via AATCC 22 spray test. Must rate 80+ (preferably 90+) before approval. Define DWR wash durability spec (must maintain 70+ after 5 washes).

size_set.check: Spray test 3 panels from size set fabric lot. Confirm DWR is active and meets spec.

pp_samples.check: Spray test on finished PP sample garment (front, back, sleeves, hood). Wash 5 cycles and re-test. Confirm curing SOP is locked.

material.check: Spray test 3 samples per incoming fabric roll (if pre-DWR-treated). Reject rolls rating below 80.
material.pass_criteria: Rating ≥ 80 on all incoming roll samples

inline_inspection.check: After DWR finishing/curing process: spray test 5 pcs per hour. Check all garment surfaces. Record spray test rating per batch.

final_inspection.check: Per AQL 1.0: spray test on all sampled garments. Test front, back, and sleeves. Record rating per garment.

packing.check: Ensure garments are not packed while damp or with oily contamination which can degrade DWR. Use clean poly bags. Check packing environment.
packing.pass_criteria: All garments packed dry and clean in poly bags

defects.name: Seam Breakage / Open Seam
defects.description: Stitched or bound seam opening under stress loads. Critical failure point at stress areas (underarm, crotch, shoulder).
defects.root_cause: Insufficient stitch density (SPI too low); wrong thread type for shell fabric; poor seam finish; tension issues.

development_samples.check: Define seam type, SPI, thread spec, and minimum seam strength in tech pack. Conduct grab test on proto seam samples — all stress seams must achieve ≥200N. Validate bound seam construction method.
development_samples.pass_criteria: All stress seams ≥ 200N grab strength. Seam type specified in tech pack.

size_set.check: Destructive grab test on 3 size set samples across underarm, shoulder, and crotch seams. Confirm production thread matches spec.
size_set.pass_criteria: Grab strength ≥ 200N on all 3 seam locations

pp_samples.check: Seam strength test on PP sample at all critical locations. Verify SPI is correct. Approve seam SOP — thread, needle, SPI, seam type all locked.
pp_samples.pass_criteria: All seams ≥ 200N. SPI 10–12 confirmed on shell. SOP signed off.

material.check: Verify incoming thread is correct type, color, ticket number as specified. Check thread tensile strength per lot COA. Reject incorrect thread.
material.pass_criteria: Thread matches spec. COA confirms tensile strength meets requirement.

sewing_stitching.check: First piece: count SPI on all seams. Grab test underarm seam. Check thread tension per machine. Every 50 pcs: recount SPI, visual open seam check on all seam lines.
sewing_stitching.pass_criteria: SPI 10–12 on all seams. No open seams. First piece grab test ≥ 200N.

assembly.check: At panel joining, tug-test all seam intersections. Bar tack placement check at underarm gusset and pocket corners. No puckering or gaping.
assembly.pass_criteria: Zero open seams. Bar tack at all stress intersections.

inline_inspection.check: Every 50 pcs: tug test on underarm, shoulder, crotch seams. Visual inspect all seam lines for skip stitches or opening. Count SPI spot check.
inline_inspection.pass_criteria: Zero open seams or skip stitches in sample

final_inspection.check: Per AQL 1.0: manual tug test on all major seams of sampled garments. Seam strength test on 5% of AQL sample (destructive). Record all seam defects on defect map.
final_inspection.pass_criteria: Zero seam failures within AQL 1.0 sample

packing.check: Final visual of all major seams (underarm, side seam) as garments are folded. Flag any visible seam issues for rework before carton close.
packing.pass_criteria: Zero open seams in packed garments

defects.name: Zipper Malfunction
defects.description: Main front zipper not running smoothly, stopping mid-travel, separating from tape, or failing to close fully.
defects.root_cause: Wrong zipper gauge; chain contamination with lint/insulation; slider not fully seated; zipper tape puckering from sewing; wrong zip spec.

development_samples.check: Define zipper brand (YKK preferred), gauge (#10 brass for Iron-Tuff®), tape width, and pull tab style in tech pack. Run 20-cycle test on proto. Slider pull test (min 15 lbs closed).
development_samples.pass_criteria: Specified: YKK #10 brass. 20-cycle smooth operation. Pull test ≥ 15 lbs.

size_set.check: Confirm zipper from approved supplier and lot. 10-cycle test on size set samples. Check slider movement and lock at top stop.
size_set.pass_criteria: Smooth 10-cycle operation. Slider locks at top stop.

pp_samples.check: 50-cycle zip test on each PP sample. Tug test at full close (15 lbs). Check boot zippers on bibs/coveralls (5 full cycles). Lock in zipper installation SOP.
pp_samples.pass_criteria: 50 smooth cycles. Pull test ≥ 15 lbs. Boot zip 5-cycle pass.

material.check: Random 5% sample from incoming zipper reels/cards: operate each zipper 5 times, check teeth alignment, slider seating, pull tab attachment.
material.pass_criteria: Zero jamming, separation, or misalignment in 5% sample

cutting.check: Not applicable. Ensure zipper tape not mixed with cut panels causing contamination.

sewing_stitching.check: First piece: operate zip 10 times after installation. Check tape is not puckered or twisted. Check teeth clearance from shell fabric. Verify chain free of lint/insulation debris.
sewing_stitching.pass_criteria: 10-cycle smooth operation. Tape flat. No debris in chain.

assembly.check: After storm flap attachment: verify flap does not interfere with zip travel. Check top and bottom stops are properly set and zip does not overshoot.
assembly.pass_criteria: Storm flap clears zip travel. Top/bottom stops engaged.

inline_inspection.check: Every garment: operate zip 10 full cycles. Check for smooth movement, no catching, no separation. Check boot zips on bibs (5 cycles each). Log failures by machine.
inline_inspection.pass_criteria: 100% pass rate. Zero jamming or separation.

final_inspection.check: Per AQL 1.0: 10-cycle zip test on all sampled garments. Pull test on 10% of sample (15 lbs). Check all secondary zips (pockets, boot zips, sleeve zips).
final_inspection.pass_criteria: Zero functional failures. Pull test ≥ 15 lbs on all tested.

packing.check: All garments must be packed with main zip closed (prevents slider damage in transit). Check zip is fully closed before folding.
packing.pass_criteria: 100% of garments have main zip fully closed in packing

defects.name: HiVis Reflective Tape Failure
defects.description: 3M™ Scotchlite™ retroreflective tape peeling at edges, cracking, losing retroreflectivity, or applied misaligned.
defects.root_cause: Improper heat-press settings; oily/contaminated substrate; wrong tape lot; substrate not pre-cleaned; tape applied on curved seam without relief cuts.

development_samples.check: Specify approved 3M Scotchlite tape type. Establish heat-press settings (temp, pressure, dwell time). Retroreflectometer test on proto (must meet ≥330 cd/lux/m²). Wash durability test (5 cycles).
development_samples.pass_criteria: Retroreflectivity ≥ 330 cd/lux/m². No peeling after 5 wash cycles.

size_set.check: Apply tape to size set sample using locked SOP. Peel test at corners. Retroreflectometer reading.

pp_samples.check: Full ANSI/ISEA 107 compliance check on PP sample. Tape placement matches spec. Retroreflectometer. Width of tape (min 2"). Length and pattern per ANSI diagram.

material.check: Verify incoming tape roll is approved 3M Scotchlite lot. Check retroreflective surface has no coating defects. Test adhesive activity by lab strip test.
material.pass_criteria: Approved tape lot confirmed. No surface defects. Adhesive active.

cutting.check: Not applicable — tape is applied after sewing, not cut.

sewing_stitching.check: Verify substrate at tape application area is clean, free of lint, oil, or release agent before sending to tape station.
sewing_stitching.pass_criteria: Clean, oil-free substrate at all tape positions

assembly.check: First piece: verify tape position vs. spec (±10mm). Check heat-press settings (temperature ± 5°C, dwell time ±2 sec). Peel-test corner of tape. Check tape is fully flat with no air bubbles or edge lift.
assembly.pass_criteria: Tape position ±10mm. No edge lift. Heat press within spec.

inline_inspection.check: Every garment: visual check all tape runs for edge adhesion. Tactile press along full tape length. Flag any bubbles, lifting edges, or misalignment. Every 25 pcs: corner peel test.
inline_inspection.pass_criteria: Zero edge lift or bubbling on any inspected garment

final_inspection.check: Per AQL 1.0: retroreflectometer reading on 10% of sample. Corner peel on all sampled garments. Tape width and placement measurement. ANSI compliance label check.
final_inspection.pass_criteria: Retroreflectivity ≥ 330. All tape placements ±10mm. ANSI label present.

packing.check: Fold garments so reflective tape is not in direct contact with other garments or carton surfaces. Use tissue paper between garments. Check tape not creased by folding.
packing.pass_criteria: Tape surfaces protected in packing. No creases on reflective surface.

defects.name: BOA® Dial Failure
defects.description: BOA® dial not ratcheting, releasing under tension, or cable snapping. Critical footwear functional failure.
defects.root_cause: Improper BOA dial installation; cable over-tensioned at factory; wrong BOA lace anchor; cable routed incorrectly.

development_samples.check: Follow BOA Technology factory installation guide for dial model. Tension test (25 lbs pull on cable). 200-cycle dial operation test. Cable routing verification against approved diagram.
development_samples.pass_criteria: 200-cycle smooth operation. Cable holds 25 lbs. Installation matches BOA guide.

size_set.check: Verify BOA cable length is appropriate for each boot size (different cable lengths per size). Test fit and dial operation across all sizes.
size_set.pass_criteria: Correct cable length per size. Full dial operation confirmed.

pp_samples.check: Submit PP sample to BOA Technology for compliance verification if required. Internal: 200-cycle test. Cable tension 25 lbs. Cold-temperature flex test (-40°F, 50 cycles).
pp_samples.pass_criteria: BOA compliance confirmed. 200-cycle pass at ambient and cold temp.

material.check: Verify incoming BOA dials and cables are correct model and from approved BOA supplier. 5% random function test on incoming components.
material.pass_criteria: Correct components confirmed. 5% sample zero function failures.

assembly.check: First pair of each style/size: BOA dial installation verification — dial orientation, cable routing path, anchor set. 20-cycle operation test. Supervisor sign-off before bulk continues.

inline_inspection.check: Every pair: 10-cycle tighten/release test on BOA dial. Check cable is not kinked or binding. Check anchor points are secure.
inline_inspection.pass_criteria: 100% pass on 10-cycle test. Zero binding or kinking.

final_inspection.check: Per AQL 1.0: 20-cycle dial test on all sampled pairs. Cable tension test (10 lbs pull) on 10% of sample. Check dial clicks distinctly at each increment.
final_inspection.pass_criteria: Zero dial failures. Cable holds 10 lbs on tested pairs.

packing.check: Pack boots in pairs with BOA dial facing inward to avoid dial being pressed against carton. Check carton compression will not damage dial during transit.
packing.pass_criteria: BOA dial protected by packing orientation

defects.name: Care Label Missing or Unreadable
defects.description: Required care and content label absent, incomplete, or unreadable. Legal and compliance requirement for all garments.
defects.root_cause: Label skipped in assembly; wrong label lot issued; label ink/print not bonding; sewing operator missed label insertion.

development_samples.check: Create approved label artwork with: fiber content (%), care symbols, country of origin, brand name, style number, size. Submit to compliance team for approval. Specify label placement in tech pack.
development_samples.pass_criteria: Label artwork compliance-approved. Placement specified in tech pack.

size_set.check: Confirm correct labels ordered per size set sizes. Verify label content matches approved artwork. Check label placement on size set samples.
size_set.pass_criteria: All labels correct content, correct placement per tech pack.

pp_samples.check: Check all labels on PP samples: care label, size label, brand label, country of origin, HiVis certification label (where applicable). Confirm all labels are legible after wash test.
pp_samples.pass_criteria: All required labels present, correct, legible. Wash legibility confirmed.

material.check: Inspect incoming label lots: compare to approved artwork (size, font, content). Check print quality — no fading, smearing, or incomplete print. Verify correct label per style.
material.pass_criteria: 100% content match to approved artwork. Print quality acceptable.

sewing_stitching.check: Ensure correct labels loaded at label-sewing stations. First piece: verify all labels inserted correctly (brand, care, size) at correct position. Supervisor check every bundle handover.
sewing_stitching.pass_criteria: First piece: all labels correct type, position, and orientation.

assembly.check: Not typically applicable unless labels added at finishing stage.

inline_inspection.check: Every 25 garments: check care label, size label, and brand label. Verify content, legibility, and position. Record any missing or wrong labels — trace back to operator.
inline_inspection.pass_criteria: Zero missing, wrong, or unreadable labels in sample

final_inspection.check: Per AQL 1.0: 100% label check on all sampled garments. Verify: brand label, care label (all symbols correct), size label, country of origin, HiVis cert label (where applicable). Photograph any failures.
final_inspection.pass_criteria: Zero label defects within AQL 1.0 sample

packing.check: Before packing: verify hang tag is attached (correct style, size, price if applicable). Confirm polybag size label sticker matches garment label. Check barcode scans correctly.
packing.pass_criteria: Hang tag present. Polybag label matches garment. Barcode scans.

defects.name: Outsole Separation
defects.description: Rubber outsole detaching from midsole or upper at the perimeter, toe, or heel.
defects.root_cause: Inadequate cement coverage; contaminated cementing surface; insufficient press time or pressure; cure time not respected.

development_samples.check: Validate two-component PU adhesive system on proto. Define roughing depth (0.5mm), primer type, cement activation temperature, and press parameters (60 PSI, 60 sec). Peel test (≥4 N/mm across full perimeter).
development_samples.pass_criteria: Peel strength ≥ 4 N/mm full perimeter. Cement SOP approved.

size_set.check: Apply approved cement SOP to size set pairs. Peel test 3 pairs (toe, heel, sides). Flex test 1,000 cycles.
size_set.pass_criteria: Peel ≥ 4 N/mm. No delamination after 1,000 flex cycles.

pp_samples.check: 10,000-cycle flex test on PP samples. Peel test after. Soak test 4 hrs then peel. Cement SOP locked for production.
pp_samples.pass_criteria: No separation after 10,000 flex cycles or 4-hr soak.

material.check: Verify incoming cement batch against approved brand/formulation. Check pot life date. Test adhesive viscosity. Inspect outsole material for contamination.
material.pass_criteria: Correct cement brand/batch. Within pot life. No outsole contamination.

sewing_stitching.check: After upper stitching: verify lasting margin is clean and free of adhesive contamination from previous operations. Check roughing of lasting edge was done per spec.
sewing_stitching.pass_criteria: Clean, properly-roughed lasting margin on all uppers

assembly.check: 100% cement coverage audit before pressing: cement must cover full outsole surface with no dry areas. Verify press machine pressure (60 PSI) and dwell time (60 sec). Monitor activation temperature. Log by operator.
assembly.pass_criteria: 100% cement coverage. 60 PSI press. 60 sec dwell. Correct activation temp.

inline_inspection.check: Every pair after pressing: visual and tactile check of full outsole perimeter for edge lift or voids. Every 30 pairs: peel test at toe and heel corners.
inline_inspection.pass_criteria: Zero edge lift. Peel test passes on corner check.

final_inspection.check: Per AQL 1.0: flex test 500 cycles on sampled pairs. Peel test at corners. Visual perimeter check on all sampled pairs. Photograph any separation.
final_inspection.pass_criteria: Zero separation after flex test. Peel test ≥ 4 N/mm.

packing.check: Do not pack boots before minimum cure time (24 hrs after pressing). Verify production date on batch record. Do not stack heavy weights on freshly cemented boots.
packing.pass_criteria: All packed boots have met minimum 24-hr cure time.

defects.name: Cut Resistance Failure
defects.description: Glove fails to meet specified ANSI/ISEA 105 cut resistance rating. Yarn substitution or degraded material used.
defects.root_cause: Wrong yarn grade sourced; supplier material substitution without notification; incorrect yarn blend ratio.

development_samples.check: Submit proto glove for ANSI/ISEA 105 TDM-100 cut test. Must achieve ANSI A4 or above per spec. Document approved yarn source and blend. Request supplier COA.
development_samples.pass_criteria: ANSI A4 confirmed by accredited lab. Yarn source documented.

size_set.check: Test size set gloves from production yarn lot. Compare COA to approved proto test results. Burn test to verify yarn composition.
size_set.pass_criteria: Size set COA matches proto. Burn test confirms yarn composition.

pp_samples.check: Submit PP sample lot (3 pairs) to accredited lab for ANSI/ISEA 105 TDM-100 test. Results must meet A4. Lab report must be on file before bulk approval.
pp_samples.pass_criteria: Accredited lab report confirms ANSI A4 on PP sample lot.

material.check: 100% COA verification on every incoming cut-resistant yarn lot. Spot destructive TDM-100 test: 1 glove per 500-pair lot minimum. HALT if yarn substitution suspected.
material.pass_criteria: COA verified. Spot test achieves ANSI A4 minimum.

cutting.check: Cut-resistant yarn rolls must be stored separately and clearly marked. Verify cut panels match approved yarn spec before issuing to sewing.
cutting.pass_criteria: Cut-resistant panels identified and segregated correctly

sewing_stitching.check: Verify cut-resistant shell is used on correct glove panels (palm, fingers, back). Check knit density matches approved sample — no substitution with standard yarn.
sewing_stitching.pass_criteria: Correct cut-resistant shell panels used per approved construction.

inline_inspection.check: Every 100 pairs: verify cut-resistant shell matches approved sample. Quick burn test on trim piece to confirm yarn type. Record yarn lot in production log.
inline_inspection.pass_criteria: Yarn confirmed as HPPE/cut-resistant type per burn test

final_inspection.check: Per AQL 1.0: visual and construction check on sample. Destructive cut test on 1% of lot (sent to lab). ANSI certification label on each glove verified.
final_inspection.pass_criteria: ANSI A4 confirmed. Zero label defects. AQL 1.0 pass.

packing.check: Verify ANSI/ISEA 105 certification information is on the glove hang tag or label. Barcode scan confirms correct SKU.
packing.pass_criteria: ANSI certification present on all packed gloves.
